text: After discovering a possible fracture, you should assess which of the following before and after splinting?

options.text: A) Capillary refill, pulse, and sensation
B) Blood pressure, heart rate, and respiratory rate
C) Level of consciousness, pupil response, and motor function

explanation: Before and after splinting any suspected fracture, it is crucial to assess the '5 Ps': Pain, Pallor, Pulses, Paresthesia (sensation), and Paralysis. Specifically, assessing distal circulation (pulse, capillary refill) and nerve function (sensation) helps ensure the splint is not too tight and does not cause further injury.
